rules.name: Object created without try/finally
rules.shortDescription: Object created but never freed (potential memory leak)
rules.fullDescription: TObject.Create (or LeakyClass.Create) without a protective try/finally block leaks the instance when subsequent code raises an exception. The Free call must run regardless of how the protected block exits.

examples.bad:
list := TStringList.Create;
DoStuff(list);   // <-- exception here leaks list

rules.name: Empty except block
rules.shortDescription: Empty except block silently swallows every exception
rules.fullDescription: An except-block with no statements catches every exception including unexpected ones (EAccessViolation, EOutOfMemory). Bugs become invisible. At minimum log the exception or re-raise.

examples.good: try DoStuff except on E: Exception do LogError(E.Message); end;

rules.name: SQL string built via concatenation
rules.shortDescription: SQL string concatenated with '+' from user-controllable input (injection risk)
rules.fullDescription: Building SQL via 'WHERE x=' + user_input enables SQL injection if the input is untrusted. Use parameterized queries (Params.ParamByName(...).Value := ...) instead.

examples.bad: Query.SQL.Text := 'SELECT * FROM Users WHERE Name=''' + UserName + '''';
examples.good:
Query.SQL.Text := 'SELECT * FROM Users WHERE Name=:n';
Query.ParamByName('n').AsString := UserName;

rules.name: Hardcoded credential / API token
rules.shortDescription: Password / API key / token as string literal in source code
rules.fullDescription: Credentials in source code end up in version control, build artifacts, decompilers, and stack traces. Move secrets to environment variables, OS credential store, or encrypted configuration.

rules.name: Format() placeholder count mismatch
rules.shortDescription: Format() / FormatUtf8() placeholder count does not match argument count
rules.fullDescription: Mismatched placeholders cause EConvertError at runtime. Detector handles RTL Format (%s/%d/...) and mORMot bare-% style (FormatUtf8/FormatString).

rules.name: File could not be read or parsed
rules.shortDescription: Parser/IO error - source file unreadable or syntactically broken
rules.fullDescription: Special-case finding (no code defect): the file could not be loaded or the lexer/parser failed. Often indicates encoding issues, includes that don't resolve, or genuine syntax errors.

examples.bad: (file is not valid UTF-8 or has lexer-breaking content)

rules.name: Unused unit in uses clause
rules.shortDescription: Uses-entry possibly unused (no identifier from it referenced)
rules.fullDescription: Heuristic: scans for any identifier from the used unit. False positives possible for units that only register classes / initialize global state via initialization sections.

examples.good: (remove if truly unused; otherwise add a comment why it stays)

rules.name: Possible nil-dereference
rules.shortDescription: Access to a variable that may be nil at this point
rules.fullDescription: Variable was assigned a value that could be nil (e.g. Find...-method returning nil) and is dereferenced without prior nil-check. Crashes with EAccessViolation at runtime.

examples.bad:
obj := FindObject(id);
obj.DoStuff;   // <-- AV if FindObject returns nil
examples.good:
obj := FindObject(id);
if Assigned(obj) then obj.DoStuff;

rules.name: Object created without protective try/finally
rules.shortDescription: .Create call without surrounding try/finally - leak risk on exception
rules.fullDescription: Similar to MemoryLeak but checked structurally: any .Create followed by code without an enclosing try/finally is flagged regardless of whether Free is called eventually.

examples.good:
obj := TFoo.Create;
try obj.DoStuff finally obj.Free end;

rules.name: Possible division by zero
rules.shortDescription: Division by a variable / expression that may be zero
rules.fullDescription: Right-hand side of div, mod, or / is a variable without prior guard against zero. Integer division crashes with EDivByZero, float division silently produces Inf/NaN.

examples.good: if count <> 0 then result := total / count;

rules.name: Code after Exit/Raise is unreachable
rules.shortDescription: Statement after Exit, raise, or Halt is dead code
rules.fullDescription: Anything after an unconditional terminator (Exit, raise, Halt, Continue, Break) in the same block is never executed. Usually leftover code from refactoring.

rules.name: Method exceeds line-count threshold
rules.shortDescription: Method longer than configured maximum (default 80 lines)
rules.fullDescription: Long methods are hard to test and understand. Threshold configurable; consider extracting helper methods or splitting responsibilities.

examples.good: (extract into ParseInput, Validate, Persist helper methods)

rules.name: Too many parameters
rules.shortDescription: Method has more parameters than configured maximum (default 7)
rules.fullDescription: High parameter counts indicate the method is doing too much. Consider grouping related parameters into a record or class.

examples.bad: procedure SaveOrder(id, customer, address, city, zip, country, total, tax, shipping: ...);

rules.name: Numeric literal without named constant
rules.shortDescription: Numeric literal in expression - extract to a named constant
rules.fullDescription: Numeric literals in business logic are unexplained. Use named constants for readability and single-point-of-change.

examples.good:
const MAX_RETRIES = 3;
if RetryCount > MAX_RETRIES then ...

rules.name: String literal repeated across multiple sites
rules.shortDescription: Same string literal appears N+ times - extract to constant
rules.fullDescription: Repeated strings are change-coupling hazards (typo in one place silently diverges from the others). Extract to a const, especially for user-facing messages.

rules.name: Filesystem path as string literal
rules.shortDescription: Hardcoded C:\ / UNC / Linux path in source
rules.fullDescription: Hardcoded paths break portability and CI deployment. Use config files, environment variables, or platform-aware path helpers (TPath.Combine, etc).

rules.name: WriteLn/ShowMessage in production code
rules.shortDescription: Debug output statement found in production unit
rules.fullDescription: WriteLn / ShowMessage / OutputDebugString usually indicate forgotten debug code. Use a proper logging framework with configurable levels.

rules.name: Block nesting exceeds threshold
rules.shortDescription: Nested if/for/while depth higher than configured maximum (default 4)
rules.fullDescription: Deep nesting hurts readability and indicates the method is doing too much. Use guard clauses (early Exit) or extract inner blocks into helper methods.

examples.bad:
if a then
  if b then
    if c then
      if d then DoStuff;

rules.name: TODO/FIXME marker in comment
rules.shortDescription: Open TODO / FIXME / HACK / XXX marker - resolve before release
rules.fullDescription: Tracks open work items embedded in source. CI can enforce zero TODOs in release branches.

examples.bad: // TODO: handle the case where input is empty
examples.good: (implement the case OR file an issue and reference it)

rules.name: Empty method body
rules.shortDescription: Method body has no statements
rules.fullDescription: Empty method may indicate a forgotten implementation, a TODO that was never followed up, or an interface stub. Make intent explicit (assert, exception, or comment).

rules.name: Duplicated code block
rules.shortDescription: Multiple identical code blocks (>= configured minimum lines)
rules.fullDescription: Detects copy-paste blocks with at least N consecutive identical lines. Extract into a helper method or shared constant.

examples.bad: (20 lines of validation code copy-pasted across 3 methods)

rules.name: Method exceeds McCabe complexity threshold
rules.shortDescription: Cyclomatic Complexity > configured threshold (default 10)
rules.fullDescription: McCabe complexity counts decision points (1 base + if + case-arm + for/while/repeat + on-handler + and/or/xor). High complexity is hard to test and understand.

examples.bad: (method with 15+ if/case/for branches in one body)
examples.good: (extract decision groups into named helper methods)

rules.name: User-defined custom rule
rules.shortDescription: Pattern matched by a rule loaded from analyser-rules.yml
rules.fullDescription: Generic kind for user-defined regex / AST rules loaded at runtime from analyser-rules.yml. Specific rule ID, message, and severity come from the YAML entry; this catalog entry is a placeholder so the dispatcher and SARIF exporter have stable metadata.

rules.name: Component with default name
rules.shortDescription: Component left at wizard-default name (Button1, Edit3, Panel2 ...)
rules.fullDescription: Default names hide intent and break find-usages / rename refactorings. Rename UI controls to convey purpose (btnSave, edUserName, pnlToolbar ...).

rules.name: Hardcoded UI text in DFM
rules.shortDescription: Caption / Hint / Text property as literal in DFM, not via i18n layer
rules.fullDescription: User-facing strings embedded in a .dfm cannot be localised, A/B-tested, or kept in a translation catalog. Assign at form construction time from a resourcestring or i18n helper.

rules.name: Hardcoded DB credentials in DFM
rules.shortDescription: Plaintext Password / ConnectionString with Pwd= on a DB component
rules.fullDescription: Database credentials persisted in a .dfm leak into version control, build artifacts, and any decompiler. Move secrets to environment variables, OS credential store, or encrypted configuration and assign at runtime.

rules.name: Duplicate (DataSource, DataField) binding
rules.shortDescription: Two or more controls bind the same (DataSource, DataField) pair
rules.fullDescription: When the user edits one bound control, the second receives a parallel update from the dataset - racey, hard-to-debug overwrites. Bind each (DataSource, DataField) to exactly one control.

rules.name: DFM event handler references missing method
rules.shortDescription: OnClick / On... points to a method that does not exist in the form class
rules.fullDescription: DFM streaming crashes at form-load time with 'class TForm has no published method X'. Usually caused by a manual rename in the .pas without updating the .dfm.

examples.bad:
object btnSave: TButton
  OnClick = btnSaveClick   // method removed in .pas
end
examples.good: (restore the method or clear OnClick in the designer)

rules.name: Orphan event handler
rules.shortDescription: Published TNotifyEvent-shaped method has no DFM binding
rules.fullDescription: Method looks like an event handler (Sender: TObject) but nothing in any .dfm references it. Likely leftover from a deleted control - remove or wire it up.

examples.bad:
procedure TForm1.btnOldClick(Sender: TObject);  // nothing binds this
begin
  ...
end;
examples.good: (remove the method or bind it from a control)

rules.name: Empty bound event handler
rules.shortDescription: Event is wired in DFM, method exists, body is empty
rules.fullDescription: An empty handler with a live DFM binding is almost always a stub forgotten after the designer added it. Either remove the binding or implement the handler.

examples.good: (implement the handler or clear OnClick in the designer)

rules.name: DFM component without published field
rules.shortDescription: Component in DFM has no matching published field in the form class
rules.fullDescription: DFM streaming requires every named component to have a corresponding published field in the host class. A missing field crashes form construction with EReadError.

examples.bad: // .dfm contains object btnSave: TButton, but TForm1 has no btnSave: TButton field
examples.good: (add the field to the published section of TForm1)

rules.name: Circular DataSource / Master-Detail loop
rules.shortDescription: Cycle in DataSource.DataSet / DataSet.MasterSource edges
rules.fullDescription: A cycle in the master-detail graph causes infinite recursion during BeforeOpen or any refresh and stack-overflows the process. Break the cycle by removing one of the links.

examples.good: (make one query the parent, the other the detail)

rules.name: SQL property built from UI input
rules.shortDescription: Query.SQL assembled from form-control Text / Caption properties
rules.fullDescription: SQL string built from form field values is SQL injection via the UI. Use parameterised queries (`ParamByName(...).AsString := EdInput.Text`).

examples.bad: FDQuery1.SQL.Text := 'SELECT * FROM U WHERE Name=''' + EdName.Text + '''';
examples.good:
FDQuery1.SQL.Text := 'SELECT * FROM U WHERE Name=:n';
FDQuery1.ParamByName('n').AsString := EdName.Text;

rules.name: Required field has no UI binding
rules.shortDescription: TField with Required=True has no bound input control
rules.fullDescription: A required field that the user cannot reach makes every insert fail with 'Field X must have a value'. Either bind a control or drop the Required=True.

examples.bad: FieldDefs: Email Required=True   // no DBEdit / DBLookup bound to it
examples.good: (bind a DBEdit to the Email field)

rules.name: Required field only on hidden controls
rules.shortDescription: TField with Required=True is bound only to Visible=False controls
rules.fullDescription: Control exists but the user cannot see or interact with it - inserts fail every time. Make at least one bound control visible or drop the Required=True.

examples.good: (make the control visible or remove Required=True)

rules.name: UI control type mismatched with TField
rules.shortDescription: DB control class does not match TField.DataType (TDBEdit for TBooleanField)
rules.fullDescription: User sees the raw value and can corrupt the type. Pick a control compatible with the field type (TDBCheckBox for booleans, TDBLookupComboBox for FKs).

rules.name: Duplicate TabOrder among siblings
rules.shortDescription: Two sibling controls in the same parent share the same TabOrder value
rules.fullDescription: VCL serialisation tolerates duplicate TabOrder but tab navigation becomes order-of-declaration dependent and unpredictable for the user. Renumber so TabOrder is unique per parent.

rules.name: Component uses forbidden class
rules.shortDescription: Component class is in the project's ForbiddenClasses list
rules.fullDescription: Style-guide enforcement for project-specific class bans (TQuery, TLabel if you have a TStyledLabel, ...). Detector stays silent unless the project sets `[Components] ForbiddenClasses=...` in analyser.ini.

rules.name: DB component on UI form
rules.shortDescription: TFDQuery / TFDConnection directly on a TForm/TFrame instead of a DataModule
rules.fullDescription: Database infrastructure on a UI form couples persistence to presentation - hard to reuse, hard to test. Move to a TDataModule and reference it from the form.

examples.bad:
object frmInvoice: TForm
  object FDQuery1: TFDQuery ...
examples.good:
object dmInvoice: TDataModule
  object FDQuery1: TFDQuery ...

rules.name: Cross-form field access
rules.shortDescription: Code in Form1 reads / writes Form2.<published_field> directly
rules.fullDescription: Reaching across forms to grab a child control breaks encapsulation - any rename in Form2 silently breaks Form1. Expose a property or method on Form2 instead.

rules.name: Input control directly on TForm
rules.shortDescription: Input control sits on the form instead of being embedded in a TPanel / TGroupBox
rules.fullDescription: Layered layout (Form > Panel > Group > Controls) makes resizing, DPI-scaling, and theming significantly easier. Wrap controls in a layout container.

examples.bad:
object frmEdit: TForm
  object EdName: TEdit  // direct child of form
examples.good:
object frmEdit: TForm
  object pnlBody: TPanel
    object EdName: TEdit

rules.name: God event handler
rules.shortDescription: Single method wired to >= N component events (default N=5)
rules.fullDescription: Spaghetti indicator: one handler dispatching dozens of events is hard to read, hard to change, and almost always has cohesion problems. Split by responsibility.

examples.bad:
btn1.OnClick = HandleEverything
btn2.OnClick = HandleEverything
btn3.OnClick = HandleEverything
btn4.OnClick = HandleEverything
btn5.OnClick = HandleEverything

rules.name: Component has Action + OnClick
rules.shortDescription: Action and OnClick both set - Action wins, OnClick is dead code
rules.fullDescription: When a TAction is assigned, VCL routes events through the action object and the OnClick never fires. Pick one or call the OnClick body from the action's OnExecute.

examples.bad:
object btnSave: TButton
  Action = actSave
  OnClick = btnSaveClick   // never fires
end

rules.name: Long string concat - prefer Format()
rules.shortDescription: Multi-segment string concatenation - extract to a Format() call
rules.fullDescription: 'a' + Var + 'b' + IntToStr(N) chains are harder to read and harder to translate than `Format('a%sb%d', [Var, N])`.

examples.bad: Msg := 'User ' + Name + ' has ' + IntToStr(N) + ' open tickets';
examples.good: Msg := Format('User %s has %d open tickets', [Name, N]);

rules.name: with X do ...
rules.shortDescription: with statement - scope-shadowing trap the compiler does not warn about
rules.fullDescription: Marco Cantu, delphi.org and Stack Overflow consistently rank `with` among the top Delphi bug sources. Identifiers from the outer scope get silently shadowed by members of the with-target. Use a local variable alias instead.

examples.bad:
with Customer do
begin
  Name := SomeName;   // Customer.Name? or local Name?
end;

rules.name: for i := High to Low - missing downto
rules.shortDescription: for i := 10 to 1 do - loop body never executes
rules.fullDescription: Classic typo: `to` instead of `downto` when iterating from high to low. The loop runs zero times. Detector flags constant From > To.

rules.name: x := x
rules.shortDescription: Self-assignment - no-op or copy-paste typo
rules.fullDescription: Detector excludes property setters with documented side effects. A bare `x := x` is almost always a typo where one side should be a different variable.

rules.name: Virtual call in constructor
rules.shortDescription: Virtual method invoked from constructor - subclass override sees half-initialised Self
rules.fullDescription: C++ FAQ 23.5 / Effective Java item 17 in Delphi form: virtual dispatch in a ctor runs the most-derived override before subclass fields are initialised. Defer to a non-virtual post-construction hook.

rules.name: Length(s) - N without guard
rules.shortDescription: Length / .Count with subtraction - native-uint underflow when empty
rules.fullDescription: `Length(s) - 1` on an empty string evaluates to 0 - 1 = MaxUInt under NativeUInt arithmetic and indexes into garbage. Guard for emptiness or cast to NativeInt.

rules.name: Public member could be private
rules.shortDescription: Public/protected member referenced only inside its own unit
rules.fullDescription: Cross-unit reference analysis: no outside caller, so tightening to private has no external impact. Reduces public API surface.

examples.bad:
public
  procedure Helper;   // only used in same unit

rules.name: Public member could be protected
rules.shortDescription: Public member referenced only from subclasses, never externally
rules.fullDescription: Cross-unit reference analysis: all external callers live in subclasses, so protected is sufficient and keeps the API narrower.

rules.name: Unused public member (dead API)
rules.shortDescription: Public member is never referenced from any subclass or cross-unit path
rules.fullDescription: No internal use AND no external use found - dead API surface. Either remove or document as intentionally exported (e.g. for binary compatibility).

rules.name: Unused local variable
rules.shortDescription: Local var declared but never referenced in method body
rules.fullDescription: Mirrors Delphi compiler hint H2164 but emitted as an SCA finding so it can be filtered, suppressed, and tracked uniformly with the other rules.

rules.name: Unused method parameter
rules.shortDescription: Method parameter is never used in the body
rules.fullDescription: Detector skips overrides, event handlers (`Sender: TObject`) and interface implementations because those signatures are externally constrained.

examples.bad: procedure Process(const Data: TData; Mode: Integer);  // Mode never referenced

rules.name: Tautological boolean expression
rules.shortDescription: Binary operator with identical LHS and RHS: x = x, a and a, (p <> p)
rules.fullDescription: Classic copy-paste bug. Either one side is wrong (the typical case - a typo) or the expression is genuinely tautological and should be removed.

rules.name: Master-Detail without MasterFields
rules.shortDescription: TDataSet has MasterSource set but no MasterFields / IndexFieldNames
rules.fullDescription: VCL silently performs a Cartesian product instead of the intended Master-Detail join - every parent row pulls every detail row at runtime. Fix by setting MasterFields (and IndexFieldNames for IB/FB).

rules.name: Form has many DB components - split DataModule
rules.shortDescription: Aggregated hint: form holds >= N DB components
rules.fullDescription: Aggregate of multiple `DfmDbInUiForm` findings on the same form - emitted as a single refactor hint instead of N individual findings.

examples.good: (move all DB components to a TDataModule)

rules.name: UPDATE / DELETE / TRUNCATE without WHERE
rules.shortDescription: SQL statement modifies every row - missing WHERE clause
rules.fullDescription: `UPDATE Users SET Active=0` without WHERE flips every row in the table. Same for `DELETE FROM ...` and `TRUNCATE TABLE ...`. Production-disaster waiting to happen.

examples.good: Q.SQL.Text := 'UPDATE Users SET Active=0 WHERE Id=:id';

rules.name: Format() float spec without TFormatSettings
rules.shortDescription: %.2f / %.3f without explicit TFormatSettings - comma vs dot decimal trap
rules.fullDescription: On a DE Windows `Format('%.2f', [3.14])` yields '3,14'; on EN-US it yields '3.14'. For machine-readable output (SQL, JSON, CSV) always pass `TFormatSettings.Invariant`.

rules.name: goto statement
rules.shortDescription: `goto` weakens structured control flow
rules.fullDescription: `goto` and labels make the control flow non-structural: loops, nesting and try/finally become hard to follow. SonarDelphi tracks the same as communitydelphi:GotoStatement. Modern Delphi code rarely justifies goto - even multi-level break is cleaner as an extracted procedure with Exit.

rules.name: Tab character in source
rules.shortDescription: Tab characters render inconsistently across editors - use spaces
rules.fullDescription: Pascal coding convention has been space-indentation since the 80s. Tabs render at 2/4/8 columns depending on the editor, break code-review side-by-side diffs and confuse alignment. SonarDelphi tracks the same as communitydelphi:TabulationCharacter.

rules.name: Source line too long
rules.shortDescription: Line exceeds 120 characters - wrap or extract subexpression
rules.fullDescription: Lines over 120 chars don't fit in a standard side-by-side code review (2*120+gutter) and overflow 16:9 monitor thirds. SonarDelphi default and Sun/Java style guides agree on 120. Threshold currently hardcoded; planned config key '[Detectors] MaxLineLength'.

examples.bad: result := SomeReallyLongFunction(ArgumentOne, ArgumentTwo, ArgumentThree, ArgumentFour, ArgumentFive);

rules.name: Trailing whitespace
rules.shortDescription: Line ends with space or tab - hygiene for diffs
rules.fullDescription: Trailing space/tab at end of line creates phantom diff lines whenever an editor with 'trim trailing whitespace on save' touches the file. SonarDelphi tracks the same as communitydelphi:TrailingWhitespace.

rules.name: Pascal keyword not lowercase
rules.shortDescription: Pascal keywords (`begin`/`end`/`procedure`/...) should be lowercase
rules.fullDescription: Object-Pascal style guide (Embarcadero DocWiki, Marco Cantu, SonarDelphi) is unambiguous: keywords are lowercase. Mixed-case variants like `Begin`/`End`/`Procedure` are leftovers from Turbo-Pascal/Delphi-1 era and create inconsistency in modern codebases. SonarDelphi tracks the same as communitydelphi:LowercaseKeyword.

rules.name: NOSONAR suppression marker
rules.shortDescription: `// NOSONAR` marker should not silence findings - audit usage
rules.fullDescription: NOSONAR markers are technical debt: each one silences a finding without fixing the underlying issue. SCA reports every NOSONAR so reviewers can audit suppressions during code review. SCA uses its own `// noinspection` marker for native suppression - NOSONAR-decorated code coming from SonarDelphi should be migrated. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:NoSonar.

examples.bad: Dispose(P); // NOSONAR - we know about this
examples.good:
// noinspection - covered by integration test
Dispose(P);

rules.name: Empty argument list
rules.shortDescription: `Foo()` should be `Foo;` - drop empty parens
rules.fullDescription: Delphi convention is to write parameterless calls without `()`. The C-style `Foo()` form adds noise without communicating anything and is inconsistent with the rest of the language (procedures, properties, function refs all omit parens when no args). Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:EmptyArgumentList.

rules.name: Inline assembly block
rules.shortDescription: `asm...end` block - prefer Pascal + compiler intrinsics
rules.fullDescription: Inline assembly creates platform lock-in (x86/x64-only bytes that don't survive ARM cross-compile or FreePascal targets), bypasses the Delphi optimizer (no inlining or reorg), and is hard to debug. The valid use cases (CPUID detection, MMX/SSE intrinsics) are covered by RTL helpers. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:InlineAssembly.

rules.name: Trailing comma in argument list
rules.shortDescription: `Foo(A, B,)` - drop the comma or add the missing argument
rules.fullDescription: Trailing comma before `)` in Delphi has no semantic benefit (unlike Python/JS where it stabilizes the diff) and suggests a forgotten argument. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:TrailingCommaArgumentList.

rules.name: Integer literal without digit grouping
rules.shortDescription: Large integer literals should use `_` separator
rules.fullDescription: Delphi 10.4+ supports `_` as a digit separator in numeric literals. Constants like timeouts (1_800_000 ms = 30 min), file sizes (1_048_576 = 1 MiB), money cents and the like are dramatically more readable with explicit grouping. Threshold is 5 digits (hardcoded; `[Detectors] DigitGroupingThreshold` is planned). Hex and float literals are exempted (different convention). Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:DigitGrouping.

rules.name: Commented-out code
rules.shortDescription: Comment looks like Pascal code - delete or document
rules.fullDescription: Heuristic: comment content with 2+ code-style markers (trailing `;`, `:=`, keywords `begin`/`end`/`procedure`/`function`/`if`/`then`/`for`/`while`) is almost certainly commented-out code. Such code rots silently: nobody dares delete it, nobody updates it. Either remove it, or replace with a TODO that explains the intent. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:CommentedOutCode.

examples.good: // TODO: re-enable once issue #123 is fixed - see history

rules.name: Unit-level keyword not at column 1
rules.shortDescription: `unit`/`interface`/`implementation`/`initialization`/`finalization` should start at column 1
rules.fullDescription: Object Pascal convention is that the structural section keywords of a unit are flush-left at column 1. Indented section keywords obscure the unit's structure when scanning and may hint at miscounted begin/end nesting. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:UnitLevelKeywordIndentation.

rules.name: Redundant boolean comparison
rules.shortDescription: `X = True` should be `X` (and `X <> False` likewise)
rules.fullDescription: Comparing a Boolean to True/False is verbose and creates subtle bugs (WinAPI BOOL where Truthy != 1 evaluates `X = True` to false). The expression itself already is the condition. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:RedundantBoolean. Note: `const X = True;` declarations are excluded (legitimate assignment, not comparison).

examples.bad:
if Active = True then ...
if Disabled <> False then ...
examples.good:
if Active then ...
if Disabled then ...

rules.name: Empty interface declaration
rules.shortDescription: Interface with no methods/properties carries no contract
rules.fullDescription: An `IFoo = interface end;` body without any methods or properties is either a refactor leftover or a marker interface in disguise. Marker interfaces are better modeled as attribute classes in Delphi. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:EmptyInterface.

rules.name: Assert without message
rules.shortDescription: `Assert(cond);` - add a `'why'` message for diagnosis
rules.fullDescription: `Assert(cond)` alone fails with the generic 'Assertion failed at $address' - no clue what was actually expected. `Assert(cond, 'why')` saves debugging hours. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:AssertMessage.

rules.name: Explicit TObject inheritance
rules.shortDescription: `class(TObject)` is redundant - drop the parens
rules.fullDescription: Every class implicitly inherits from TObject; `TFoo = class(TObject)` is identical to `TFoo = class`. Removing the explicit base highlights real inheritance changes in diffs. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:ExplicitTObjectInheritance.

rules.name: Grouped variable / field / parameter declaration
rules.shortDescription: Split `A, B: Type` into one declaration per line
rules.fullDescription: Grouped declarations (`A, B, C: Integer;` for vars, fields or parameters) confuse diffs (a new variable changes an existing line), prevent per-variable comments, and complicate type-change refactors. Each declaration on its own line is cleaner. Unifies SonarDelphi communitydelphi:GroupedFieldDeclaration / :GroupedVariableDeclaration / :GroupedParameterDeclaration into one rule (SCA may split later).

examples.bad:
var A, B, C: Integer;
field FX, FY: TPoint;

rules.name: Empty begin..end block
rules.shortDescription: Empty `begin..end` - delete it or fill in the statement
rules.fullDescription: An empty `begin..end` is almost always a refactor leftover or never-filled-in placeholder. Either delete it or implement the missing logic. Top-level unit `begin end.` initialization sections are exempted (that is the empty init form, not a refactor rest). Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:EmptyBlock.

rules.name: Catch-all on root Exception class
rules.shortDescription: `on E: Exception do` swallows everything including AV/OOM
rules.fullDescription: Catching the root `Exception` class catches every error including unrecoverable ones (EAccessViolation, EOutOfMemory, EStackOverflow). Prefer a specific exception type (EDatabaseError, EFOpenError, EConvertError, ...) so unrelated failures aren't masked. Suppress with `// noinspection` if the catch-all is intentional (e.g. logger boundary).

examples.bad: try Foo; except on E: Exception do Log(E.Message); end;
examples.good: try Foo; except on E: EDatabaseError do Log(E.Message); end;

rules.name: Consecutive const/type/var section
rules.shortDescription: Two `const`/`type`/`var` blocks in a row should be merged
rules.fullDescription: `const A = 1; const B = 2;` should be `const A = 1; B = 2;` - one section keyword can declare all of them. Same for `type` and `var`. Unifies SonarDelphi communitydelphi:ConsecutiveConstSection / :ConsecutiveTypeSection / :ConsecutiveVarSection into one rule. The section type is in the message.

rules.name: Redundant Exit/Continue/Break before end
rules.shortDescription: `Exit;` / `Continue;` / `Break;` directly before `end` is a no-op
rules.fullDescription: If `Exit` is the last statement of a procedure (or `Continue`/`Break` the last in a loop body), control flow already leaves the block - the jump statement is dead. Delete it to clarify intent. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:RedundantJump.

rules.name: Multiple class declarations in one file
rules.shortDescription: One class per unit makes refactoring easier
rules.fullDescription: When a unit declares two or more public classes, finding and moving them between units is harder than necessary. SonarDelphi communitydelphi:ClassPerFile suggests the same. Forward declarations (`TFoo = class;`) and class-reference types (`TFooClass = class of TFoo`) are exempted.

rules.name: Double semicolon
rules.shortDescription: `;;` - drop the extra semicolon
rules.fullDescription: A double semicolon (`;;`) usually means a forgotten typo or a stray paste leftover. Compilers treat the second `;` as an empty statement, so it doesn't break, but it adds noise. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:SuperfluousSemicolon.

rules.name: Empty finally block
rules.shortDescription: `try ... finally end;` has no cleanup - either add it or drop the finally
rules.fullDescription: An empty `finally` block is almost always a forgotten cleanup. If there really is nothing to clean up, drop the `try..finally` wrapper. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:EmptyFinallyBlock.

rules.name: Redundant Assigned + nil check
rules.shortDescription: `Assigned(X) and (X <> nil)` - drop the nil check
rules.fullDescription: `Assigned(X)` for pointers and class instances is semantically identical to `X <> nil`. Combining both is redundant - the second check can never give a different result. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:AssignedAndAssignedNil.

rules.name: X.Free; X := nil; should be FreeAndNil(X)
rules.shortDescription: Use `FreeAndNil(X)` instead of `X.Free; X := nil;`
rules.fullDescription: `FreeAndNil(X)` is the canonical Delphi idiom: it does both atomically and avoids a dangling pointer if `X.Free` raises (e.g. destructor failure). Manual two-line form is also more diff-noisy and easier to break apart during later refactors. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:FreeAndNil.

rules.name: Avoid out parameter modifier
rules.shortDescription: Prefer `var` over `out` (out has surprising semantics)
rules.fullDescription: `out` parameters clear managed types (string/Interface/dynamic arrays) on method entry - the caller's value is discarded before the method runs. Record/simple types stay uninitialized, so reading the incoming value is UB. Both are rarely what was intended. `var` is the safer default unless you need the COM-interop semantics. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:AvoidOut.

rules.name: Empty visibility section in class
rules.shortDescription: `public`/`private`/... section header with no members
rules.fullDescription: A class with a visibility keyword followed immediately by another visibility keyword (or `end`) is usually a refactor leftover - all members of that section moved elsewhere. Delete the empty header. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:EmptyVisibilitySection.

rules.name: Legacy unit-init begin..end.
rules.shortDescription: Use `initialization..end.` instead of legacy `begin..end.`
rules.fullDescription: Pre-Delphi-2 units marked their init block with `begin..end.`. Since Delphi 2 the idiomatic form is `initialization..end.` which can be paired with an optional `finalization` block for symmetric cleanup. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:LegacyInitializationSection.

rules.name: Public field in class
rules.shortDescription: Public field breaks encapsulation - use a property
rules.fullDescription: A public field exposes the underlying storage; callers can mutate it without the class noticing. A property gives the class the option to add validation/normalization later without breaking callers. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:PublicField.

rules.name: Nested try block
rules.shortDescription: Nested `try..end` - consider extracting inner try into a method
rules.fullDescription: Nesting try blocks makes the control flow of cleanup / error recovery harder to read. Most cases unwind by either re-ordering the cleanups or extracting the inner block into its own method. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:NestedTry. The detector uses a depth heuristic (counting `try` vs `end` keywords) and may produce a few false positives in heavily nested begin/end code.

examples.good: ProtectedDoStuff;  // helper wraps the inner try

rules.name: Large case statement
rules.shortDescription: `case` with >= 10 branches - consider polymorphism / dispatch table
rules.fullDescription: A `case` with many branches usually hides a polymorphism or strategy pattern. The code would be more open/closed (new behavior = new class, not new branch) and the file shorter when each branch becomes its own class method or a `TDictionary<Key, TProc>` entry. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:CaseStatementSize. Threshold currently hardcoded; `[Detectors] MaxCaseBranches` is planned.

rules.name: Unit contains no declarations
rules.shortDescription: Unit has no type/const/var/procedure/function - delete or fill in
rules.fullDescription: A `.pas` file with a unit header but no declarations is dead weight. Either delete the file or fill in the placeholder. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:EmptyFile.

rules.name: Multiple inherited calls in one method
rules.shortDescription: Two or more `inherited;` in the same method - parent side-effects run twice
rules.fullDescription: Each `inherited` invocation runs the parent implementation. Two calls fire side-effects (event notifications, refcount updates, log lines) twice. Almost always a copy-paste bug. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:TwiceInheritedCalls.

rules.name: Redundant double parentheses
rules.shortDescription: `((Ident))` - drop the outer parens
rules.fullDescription: Double parentheses around a single identifier, literal or simple expression add nothing - a single pair is sufficient (often none). Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:RedundantParentheses. Only the simple-expression case is flagged; complex `((A + B))` may still benefit from inner parens for precedence and is not reported.

rules.name: Consecutive visibility section
rules.shortDescription: Same `public`/`private`/etc. section appears twice in one class
rules.fullDescription: Two `private` (or `public`/`protected`/`published`) sections in the same class with members between them should be merged into one. Helps reviewers grasp class shape at a glance. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:ConsecutiveVisibilitySection. Note: empty header followed by another header is the related but distinct `EmptyVisibilitySection` rule (SCA087).

rules.name: Constructor without inherited call
rules.shortDescription: Constructor missing `inherited Create` - parent stays uninitialized
rules.fullDescription: A constructor that does not call `inherited Create(...)` (or `inherited;`) skips parent-class initialization. Parent fields and registrations end up at default/nil state, which leads to follow-up crashes when methods expect the parent to be ready. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:ConstructorWithoutInherited.

rules.name: Destructor without inherited call
rules.shortDescription: Destructor missing `inherited Destroy` - parent cleanup is skipped (leak risk)
rules.fullDescription: A destructor without `inherited Destroy` (or `inherited;`) prevents the parent class from releasing its own fields, unregistering handlers, and updating reference counts. Almost always a leak. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:DestructorWithoutInherited.

rules.name: Redundant conditional assignment
rules.shortDescription: `if Cond then X := True else X := False` should be `X := Cond`
rules.fullDescription: Two branches assigning True/False to the same variable can be reduced to a single direct assignment (or `not Cond` if the polarity is reversed). Shorter, faster, and matches the boolean expression more directly. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:RedundantConditional.

examples.bad: if Active then Result := True else Result := False;

rules.name: Asymmetric begin/end in if/else
rules.shortDescription: then-branch uses `begin..end` but else-branch is a single statement
rules.fullDescription: When the then-branch uses `begin..end`, the else-branch should too (or both should drop it). The mixed form (`end else DoStuff;`) makes a future reader unsure whether more statements belong in the else-branch but were forgotten. `else if` (else-if chain) and `else begin` are explicitly allowed. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:IfElseBegin.

rules.name: Pointer type alias not prefixed with P
rules.shortDescription: `Foo = ^Bar` should be `PBar = ^Bar` (P-prefix convention)
rules.fullDescription: Delphi convention since day one: a pointer alias on `TXxx` is named `PXxx`. The `P` prefix makes the indirection visible at the call site. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:PointerName. Naming framework will later make the prefix configurable.

rules.name: Branch without begin..end block
rules.shortDescription: `then`/`else`/`do <stmt>` - prefer explicit `begin..end`
rules.fullDescription: A single-statement branch without `begin..end` is easy to misread; adding a second statement later without re-blocking is a classic source of indentation-vs-semantics bugs. Style-debated rule (many Delphi codebases use the compact form deliberately) - lsHint, easy to disable in profiles. `else if` chain, `raise`/`exit`/`break`/`continue` and other block-opening statements are explicitly allowed. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:BeginEndRequired.

rules.name: Nested routine inside another method
rules.shortDescription: Local nested procedure/function - extract to unit-level
rules.fullDescription: Nested routines (a `procedure`/`function` declared inside the local-decl section of another routine) are syntactically allowed but discourage testing and reuse: the inner routine can't be unit-tested or reused from elsewhere. Modern Delphi prefers anonymous methods (lambdas) for the rare cases the nesting was actually needed. Anonymous methods are NOT flagged (they have no name in the AST). Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:NestedRoutines.

rules.name: Class field not prefixed with F
rules.shortDescription: Class fields should follow `F<Name>` convention
rules.fullDescription: Delphi convention: class instance fields are named `FFoo` so the reader instantly knows they refer to a class member (vs a local variable or parameter). The `F` prefix avoids naming clashes with public properties (`FFoo` field with `Foo` property is the canonical pair). Only `private` and `protected` field declarations are checked - public/published fields are caught by `PublicField` (SCA089) anyway. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:FieldName.

rules.name: Class/record type not prefixed with T
rules.shortDescription: Class and record type aliases should start with `T`
rules.fullDescription: Delphi convention: class types `TXxx`, records `TXxx` or `RXxx`, interfaces `IXxx`, pointers `PXxx` (SCA100), exceptions `EXxx`. This detector covers the class/record subset only. Forward declarations (`TFoo = class;`), `class of` references (`TFooClass = class of TFoo`) and generic syntax are handled. Matches SonarDelphi communitydelphi:TypeName.
